utility_structure.source: Main text Eq. (1), Table 4, and Appendix A utility functions

respondent_microdata.assumption: Synthetic respondents are sampled from public Table 1 margins plus explicit approximations for household cars, accessible bikes, and MaaS subscription.

ngene_blocks.assumption: Eight SD blocks and eight MD blocks are reconstructed from the published attribute levels using deterministic cyclic balancing rules. Choice-set combinations are marked inferred_from_public.

questionnaire_language.assumption: The paper's English survey description is used to reconstruct the survey briefing. LLM-formatting instructions are added as implementation scaffolding.

subsample_assignment.paper_statement: Respondents were randomly selected into SD or MD sub-samples.

block_assignment.implementation: Each respondent is randomly assigned one reconstructed block within the sampled sub-sample using the master seed.

task_ordering.paper_statement: Choice situations shown to the respondents are randomly drawn from one block.
task_ordering.implementation: The six tasks from the assigned block are shown in respondent-specific random order.

maas_subscription.reason: The pooled utility specification includes MaaS, but the public paper does not report its sample share.

scale_correction.implementation: PHI_POOL enters the private-car utility exactly as shown in Appendix A.
